site.title: NRL TipBot Briefing 2026
site.subhead: Round 9 wrap board for tipping, charity betting, and weekly NRL intel
site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner bet tracking, and banking any wins for charity.

meta.description: Codex-managed NRL TipBot dashboard for Round 9 preview notes, charity betting discipline, and weekly NRL intel.

ticker: round 9 finished better than it looked on saturday thanks to a clean 3 from 3 sunday
the final card landed 4 from 8 which is not pretty but better than the one from five hole
the mark hughes foundation ledger stays at 2 and 2 and minus $3.50 after the $10 bulldogs miss
storm have now lost seven straight and stay well outside the trust circle
the knights, sharks and panthers rescue means the process did not totally fall apart
round 10 should be a reset week once fresh team lists arrive

featurePanels.title: Lock Pick
items: The bank stays at -$3.50 after the $10 loss
The important win was process discipline after Friday
Sunday landing 3 from 3 kept the round from turning into a total collapse
Round 10 now deserves a calmer reset

featurePanels.title: Risk Flags
items: Storm have now lost seven straight and cannot be priced on reputation anymore
The Bulldogs are still too error-prone to wear the clean-tip label
Broncos volatility remains real even with stars back
The Tigers and Rabbitohs both showed how fragile hot-form reads can be
Do not let one rescue Sunday hide the weak reads from Friday and Saturday

featurePanels.title: Weekly Focus
items: Carry the Sunday rescue energy forward without pretending the round was good
Use the Knights return story as a reminder that team-news spikes still matter
Keep Penrith and Cronulla in the reliable bucket until proven otherwise
Treat Melbourne as broken until they actually stop losing
Approach Round 10 as a reset, not a rebound chase

featurePanels.title: Watchlist
items: Round 10 team lists when they arrive
Whether the next charity bet should go back to smaller stakes
How much faith to keep in Newcastle after the full-strength rebound
Which Round 9 errors were process mistakes versus pure chaos

betCard.title: Round 9 Charity Bet
why: Canterbury still look more settled top to bottom and should control the game better.
North Queensland lose Murray Taulagi and have to reshuffle again out wide.
At $1.77 on the TAB board, the number looked playable enough to take the shot.
risks: The Bulldogs are without Viliame Kikau and Harry Hayes, so they are not at full strength either.
Tom Dearden and Drinkwater can still drag the Cowboys into chaos-ball contests.
The Bulldogs were still too error-prone and the Cowboys punished it.

charity.title: Mark Hughes Foundation Ledger
charity.summary: All profits are earmarked for the Mark Hughes Foundation.
notes: Round 5: Newcastle head to head at $2.30 won and collected $11.50.
Round 6: North Queensland Cowboys head to head at $2.00 won and collected $10.00.
Round 7: Wests Tigers head to head at $1.53 lost in a 21-20 defeat to Brisbane.
Round 8: No charity bet placed, bank held at $6.50.
Round 9: Bulldogs head to head at $1.77 lost after a Cowboys comeback win.

watchlist: Whether the Bulldogs are still playable once the market settles.
How much the Broncos and Knights improve with key names back on deck.
Any value opening in Panthers-Manly if people over-fade Turbo's absence.
Which Round 8 reads still carry forward once the new team lists hit.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 9 ended less badly than it started
lines: As of Monday, 4 May 2026, Round 9 has closed at 4 from 8 after a much better Sunday than the Friday-Saturday damage suggested.
The Bulldogs charity play still hurt and the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger stays at 2-2 and -$3.50.
But the clean 3 from 3 finish on Sunday means the process bent without fully breaking.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: The charity miss stands, but Sunday stopped the bleeding
lines: The Bulldogs still looked like a playable price on paper, but their sloppiness stayed alive long enough for North Queensland to punish it.
That loss still matters because it flips the charity ledger back into the red at -$3.50.
The saving grace is that Knights, Sharks and Panthers all landed on Sunday, which kept the tipping side from turning into a full disaster.
The key now is to take the rescue without forgetting the lesson.

sections.title: Round 9 Tips
sections.heading: Final card logged for review
lines: Titans over Raiders
Bulldogs over Cowboys
Warriors over Eels
Storm over Dolphins
Broncos over Roosters
Knights over Rabbitohs
Sharks over Wests Tigers
Panthers over Sea Eagles

sections.title: Next Step
sections.heading: Best workflow before the Round 10 rebuild
lines: Log Round 9 as a 4 from 8 week.
Carry the charity ledger forward at -$3.50.
Wait for Round 10 team lists before building new leans.
Do a harsher review on Friday-Saturday reads than on the Sunday rescue picks.

sections.title: Form Radar
sections.heading: What Round 9 actually taught us
lines: Hot: Panthers, Sharks and a healthier Knights side.
Cold: Storm, Bulldogs trust, and any assumption that Broncos returns automatically fix them.
The biggest upgrade from the round is respect for the Sunday backbone of the card.

sections.title: Intel Notes
sections.heading: What to carry into Round 10 analysis
lines: Storm stay fully outside the trust bucket until they actually win again.
The Knights star returns were real and need to be valued properly next week.
The Bulldogs still need to prove they can close games cleanly before they become another charity play.
The next bet should probably go back to a smaller, cleaner number rather than another stretched stake.

footerNotes: Built from the attached Claude prototype and reorganised into a maintainable Codex workflow.
Use this as a weekly operating board, then swap in live-verified NRL data when you want to publish or bet.
